content_en:
Al-Qarni's work is distinguished from purely theoretical treatments of Islamic psychology by its abundance of practical, concrete, immediately applicable guidance. This chapter catalogues the specific actions, practices, and behavioral changes that the author recommends for people struggling with sadness, grief, and depression — rooted in Islamic sources but expressed in accessible, direct language.

The first and most important practical step is prayer — and specifically, the two-rak'ah prayer of distress that the Prophet explicitly prescribed for moments of difficulty. The companions reported that whenever the Prophet faced a difficult matter, he would turn to prayer. Ibn Abbas narrated that the Prophet said: 'If you are afflicted with something difficult, then pray.' This is not a passive waiting for divine intervention but an active turning toward the only source of genuine help and comfort — a deliberate act of reorientation that interrupts the spiral of anxious thought and places one's situation in the context of the divine relationship.

Supplication (dua) is the companion to prayer and perhaps the most immediately accessible tool. Al-Qarni presents the specific supplications that the Prophet prescribed for distress and anxiety, including the famous supplication: 'O Allah, I am Your servant, son of Your servant, son of Your handmaid; my forelock is in Your hand; Your command concerning me prevails; Your decree concerning me is just. I beseech You by every name that You have named Yourself, or revealed in Your Book, or taught to any of Your creation, or reserved in the knowledge of the unseen with You — make the Quran the spring of my heart, the light of my chest, the banisher of my sadness and anxiety.' This dua — which the Prophet described as guaranteed to replace sadness with joy when recited sincerely — is both a prayer and an act of trust in Allah's ability to change one's inner state.

Physical action is another practical tool. Al-Qarni notes that the depressed person typically retreats into inactivity — avoiding social contact, abandoning pleasurable activities, withdrawing from responsibilities. This withdrawal deepens the depression in a vicious cycle. The Islamic prescription is almost the opposite: maintain your prayers, fulfill your obligations, engage with your family and community, continue useful work. The Prophet never prescribed self-indulgent withdrawal from life as a response to grief; the prophetic model was of continued engagement with one's mission and relationships even in periods of personal difficulty.

Service to others is highlighted as one of the most effective prescriptions for sadness. The person who is absorbed in their own pain finds relief in turning outward — in visiting a sick neighbor, helping a struggling family member, contributing to a community project, or simply offering a sincere smile to those they encounter. The Prophet's famous saying — 'None of you has faith until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself' — expresses a social orientation that, when practiced, naturally pulls the self out of its inward absorption.

Reading and learning are presented as powerful antidotes to the rumination that characterizes prolonged sadness. The engagement of the intellect with beneficial knowledge — particularly Quranic reflection, Islamic scholarship, and the biographies of the prophets and scholars — provides an alternative focus for the mind's activity and fills the consciousness with perspectives that naturally address despair. Al-Qarni encourages his readers to maintain a discipline of regular Islamic reading regardless of their emotional state, understanding that the benefits accumulate over time even when no immediate mood improvement is felt.

Finally, al-Qarni advocates for seeking supportive human company — the company of righteous people whose own faith is strong and whose presence is naturally uplifting. The Prophet described the good companion as one like the seller of musk: even if you buy nothing from them, you come away carrying the fragrance of their presence. Deliberate cultivation of such companionship — and deliberate avoidance of the company that amplifies sadness, resentment, and despair — is one of the most practically effective steps available.
content_id:
Karya al-Qarni dibedakan dari perlakuan-perlakuan teoritis murni tentang psikologi Islam oleh kelimpahannya dengan panduan praktis, konkret, dan langsung dapat diterapkan. Bab ini mendaftar tindakan-tindakan, amalan-amalan, dan perubahan-perubahan perilaku spesifik yang direkomendasikan penulis bagi orang-orang yang berjuang dengan kesedihan, duka, dan depresi — berakar pada sumber-sumber Islam tetapi diekspresikan dalam bahasa yang mudah diakses dan langsung.

Langkah praktis pertama dan terpenting adalah shalat — dan khususnya, shalat dua rakaat kesusahan yang Nabi ﷺ secara eksplisit tetapkan untuk momen-momen kesulitan. Para sahabat melaporkan bahwa setiap kali Nabi ﷺ menghadapi hal yang sulit, beliau akan beralih ke shalat. Ibn Abbas meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika kamu ditimpa sesuatu yang sulit, maka shalatlah.' Ini bukan menunggu secara pasif akan intervensi ilahi tetapi berpaling secara aktif menuju satu-satunya sumber bantuan dan penghiburan yang sejati — tindakan reorientasi yang disengaja yang menginterupsi spiral pikiran cemas dan menempatkan situasi seseorang dalam konteks hubungan ilahi.

Doa (du'a) adalah pendamping shalat dan mungkin alat yang paling langsung dapat diakses. Al-Qarni menyajikan doa-doa spesifik yang Nabi ﷺ tetapkan untuk tekanan dan kecemasan, termasuk doa terkenal: 'Ya Allah, aku adalah hamba-Mu, putra hamba-Mu, putra hamba perempuan-Mu; ubun-ubunku ada di tangan-Mu; perintah-Mu berkaitan denganku berlaku; takdir-Mu berkaitan denganku adil. Aku memohon kepada-Mu dengan setiap nama yang Engkau namai diri-Mu, atau Engkau wahyukan dalam Kitab-Mu, atau Engkau ajarkan kepada siapapun dari makhluk-Mu, atau Engkau simpan dalam pengetahuan gaib bersama-Mu — jadikanlah Al-Quran sebagai musim semi hatiku, cahaya dadaku, pengusir kesedihan dan kecemasanku.' Doa ini — yang Nabi ﷺ gambarkan dijamin menggantikan kesedihan dengan kegembiraan ketika dibaca dengan tulus — adalah sekaligus doa dan tindakan kepercayaan pada kemampuan Allah untuk mengubah keadaan batin seseorang.

Tindakan fisik adalah alat praktis lainnya. Al-Qarni mencatat bahwa orang yang depresi biasanya mundur ke dalam ketidakaktifan — menghindari kontak sosial, meninggalkan aktivitas-aktivitas yang menyenangkan, menarik diri dari tanggung jawab. Penarikan diri ini memperdalam depresi dalam siklus setan. Resep Islam hampir berlawanan: pertahankan shalatmu, penuhi kewajibanmu, terlibatlah dengan keluarga dan komunitasmu, lanjutkan pekerjaan yang bermanfaat. Nabi ﷺ tidak pernah meresepkan penarikan diri dari kehidupan yang memanjakan diri sebagai respons terhadap duka; model nabawi adalah keterlibatan berkelanjutan dengan misi dan hubungan seseorang bahkan di masa-masa kesulitan pribadi.

Pelayanan kepada orang lain disorot sebagai salah satu resep paling efektif untuk kesedihan. Orang yang terserap dalam rasa sakitnya sendiri menemukan keringanan dengan berpaling ke luar — dalam mengunjungi tetangga yang sakit, membantu anggota keluarga yang berjuang, berkontribusi pada proyek komunitas, atau sekadar menawarkan senyum tulus kepada mereka yang ditemui. Sabda terkenal Nabi ﷺ — 'Tidak ada salah satu dari kalian yang beriman sampai ia mencintai untuk saudaranya apa yang ia cintai untuk dirinya sendiri' — mengungkapkan orientasi sosial yang, ketika dipraktikkan, secara alami menarik diri keluar dari penyerapan batinnya.

Membaca dan belajar disajikan sebagai penawar kuat terhadap perenungan yang menjadi ciri kesedihan berkepanjangan. Keterlibatan akal dengan pengetahuan yang bermanfaat — terutama refleksi Al-Quran, keilmuan Islam, dan biografi para nabi dan ulama — menyediakan fokus alternatif untuk aktivitas pikiran dan mengisi kesadaran dengan perspektif-perspektif yang secara alami mengatasi keputusasaan. Al-Qarni mendorong pembacanya untuk mempertahankan disiplin membaca Islam secara teratur tanpa memandang keadaan emosi mereka, memahami bahwa manfaatnya terakumulasi dari waktu ke waktu bahkan ketika tidak ada peningkatan suasana hati yang segera dirasakan.

Akhirnya, al-Qarni mengadvokasi pencarian pergaulan manusia yang suportif — pergaulan orang-orang saleh yang imannya sendiri kuat dan kehadirannya secara alami mengangkat semangat. Nabi ﷺ menggambarkan teman yang baik seperti penjual minyak wangi: bahkan jika kamu tidak membeli apapun dari mereka, kamu pergi membawa wangi kehadirannya. Pengembangan persahabatan semacam itu yang disengaja — dan penghindaran sengaja terhadap pergaulan yang memperkuat kesedihan, kebencian, dan keputusasaan — adalah salah satu langkah yang paling efektif secara praktis yang tersedia.
content_ar: Al-Qarni's work is distinguished from purely theoretical treatments of Islamic psychology by its abundance of practical, concrete, immediately applicable الهداية. This الباب catalogues the specific actions, practices, and behavioral changes that the author recommends for people struggling with الحزن, الحزن, and الاكتئاب — rooted in Islamic sources but expressed in accessible, direct language. The أولاً and most المهم practical step is الصلاة — and specifically, the two-rak'ah الصلاة of distress that النبي ﷺ explicitly prescribed for moments of difficulty. The الصحابة reported that كلما النبي ﷺ faced a difficult matter, he would turn to الصلاة. ابن عباس narrated that النبي ﷺ said: 'إذا you are afflicted with something difficult, then pray.' This is not a passive waiting for divine intervention but an active turning toward the only source of genuine help and comfort — a deliberate act of reorientation that interrupts the spiral of anxious thought and places one's situation in the context of the divine relationship. الدعاء (الدعاء) is the companion to الصلاة and perhaps the most immediately accessible tool. Al-Qarni presents the specific supplications that النبي ﷺ prescribed for distress and القلق, بما في ذلك the famous الدعاء: 'O الله, I am Your servant, son of Your servant, son of Your handmaid; my forelock is in Your hand; Your command فيما يتعلق بـ me prevails; Your decree فيما يتعلق بـ me is just. I beseech You by every name that You have named Yourself, or revealed in Your الكتاب, or taught to any of Your creation, or reserved in the العلم of the unseen with You — make the القرآن الكريم the spring of my القلب, the light of my chest, the banisher of my الحزن and القلق.' This الدعاء — which النبي ﷺ described as guaranteed to replace الحزن with joy عندما recited sincerely — is both a الصلاة and an act of trust in الله's ability to change one's inner state. Physical action is another practical tool. Al-Qarni notes that the depressed person typically retreats into inactivity — avoiding social contact, abandoning pleasurable activities, withdrawing from responsibilities. This withdrawal deepens the الاكتئاب in a vicious cycle. The Islamic prescription is almost the opposite: maintain your الصلوات, fulfill your obligations, engage with your الأسرة and الجماعة, continue useful work. النبي ﷺ never prescribed self-indulgent withdrawal from الحياة as a response to الحزن; the prophetic model was of continued engagement with one's mission and relationships even in periods of personal difficulty. Service to others is highlighted as one of the most effective prescriptions for الحزن. The person who is absorbed in their own الألم finds الفرج in turning outward — in visiting a sick الجار, helping a struggling الأسرة member, contributing to a الجماعة project, or simply offering a sincere smile to those they encounter. النبي ﷺ's famous saying — 'None of you has الإيمان حتى he loves for his الأخ what he loves for himself' — expresses a social orientation that, عندما practiced, naturally pulls the self out of its inward absorption. Reading and learning are presented as powerful antidotes to the rumination that characterizes prolonged الحزن. The engagement of the العقل with beneficial العلم — particularly القرآني reflection, Islamic scholarship, and the biographies of the الأنبياء and العلماء — provides an alternative focus for the العقل's activity and fills the consciousness with perspectives that naturally address despair. Al-Qarni encourages his readers to maintain a discipline of regular Islamic reading regardless of their emotional state, الفهم that the benefits accumulate over time even عندما no immediate mood improvement is felt. وأخيراً, al-Qarni advocates for seeking supportive human company — the company of الصالح people whose own الإيمان is strong and whose presence is naturally uplifting. النبي ﷺ described the الخير companion as one like the seller of musk: even إذا you buy nothing from them, you come away carrying the fragrance of their presence. Deliberate cultivation of such companionship — and deliberate avoidance of the company that amplifies الحزن, resentment, and despair — is one of the most practically effective steps available.
